# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: The Riftbreaker
- Steam AppID of the game: 780310

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 Ti
- Driver/LLVM version: 4.6.0 NVIDIA 510.54
- Kernel version: 5.17.0-2-MANJARO
- [Link to full system information report as Gist](https://gist.github.com/Diman0/1d23c8c7451b2bd59d65e0bb899b9f1b):
- Proton version: 7.0.1

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Intermittently the game crashes to desktop. I attached the logs of the last event. I'm not doing anything special at this moment.

Another problem is that the configuration tool cannot be started. It reports a critical error with stack trace on startup.

## Reproduction
CTD during gampleay: Play the game. It will happen eventually. It is not possible to play 1+ hour without a CTD. 

CTD configuration utlity: Start the game and choose Configuration utility. The crash immediately happens.

Marvel's Spider-Man Remastered (1817070): Random Crashes During Normal Gameplay

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/6090.
@Javillin posted on 2022-08-14T00:03:24:

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Marvel's Spider-Man Remastered
- Steam AppID of the game: 1817070

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1080 Ti 
- Driver/LLVM version: NVIDIA 515.65.01
- Kernel version: 5.19.1-2-MANJARO
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Javillin/369d9e689299e90f991c8fdf3c7da99c
- Proton version: 1659678174 experimental-7.0-20220805 [Proton Experimental]

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
The game will crash at random during normal game play. Sometimes I can get away with a couple hours of consistent game play and other times it crashes within a few minutes, but the same issue will happen every time.

The game will freeze while audio continues playing. I will have to alt+tab out of the game to force close it with steam. I have not done anything other than play the game to get the crashes and it doesn't matter if I play the story missions or side missions or just collect things around the world.

It seems to happen at random in the open world and sometimes when I transition from open world to inside a building for a mission. Everything else runs smooth and I have no issues other than random crashes at different times during game play.

Changing the graphics settings does not help and sometimes will cause the game to crash if graphic settings are changed during game play.
## Reproduction
Will crash all the time during gameplay. 

[steam-1817070.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9332438/steam-1817070.log)

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Metro Exodus Enhanced Edition
- Steam AppID of the game: 412020 / 1449560

## System Information
- GPU: 6800XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.1.3
- Kernel version: 5.12.13-zen
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/sankasan/7db37a1a6be43f0450f556e55069dd5f
- Proton version: Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms
Metro Exodus Enhanced Edition does show the startup videos play, but presents a black screen after that. It shows a popup informing the user that the game has crashed. Crash is detected and the user can save a crashdump.

Game crashes most likely due to lack of raytracing support on AMD/RADV.

## Reproduction
Start game on AMD 6800XT with RADV driver

- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Log
[steam-1743850.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10558890/steam-1743850.log)

## Symptoms 
Game works fine for the most part, the only issue is when making a clip, the game crashes to desktop. I believe it's related to the media foundation h264 encoder but I can't say for sure.

## Reproduction
1) View a replay for a run, by either playing the game once or viewing a previous one
2) Click "MAKE CLIP", optionally select a region
3) Click Upload

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Creatures Docking Station
- Steam AppID of the game: 1659050

## System Information
- GPU: Intel HD Graphics 4000 (IVB GT2)
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.2.2
- Kernel version: 5.15.5-76051505-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/truboxl/d89d8496d53cea55cb358acaa5c00ac2
- Proton version: Proton Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Unable to launch the game. Getting the error:

"The game can not continue: Please change your display settings to be 'High Color (16 bit)'"

![Screenshot from 2021-12-20 22-23-09](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12658589/146782160-a72cc44c-b8d9-4c68-9868-d935b429062f.png)

## Reproduction
Simply launch the game and choose "Play Creatures Docking Station"

## Notes
There simply no easy way to set 16bit color depth in Linux.

Wine suggest we have to fiddle with `/etc/X11/xorg.conf.d/` or `/etc/X11/xorg.conf` and set something along the lines of `DefaultDepth    16`. But that will cause Steam not to start. See https://wiki.winehq.org/FAQ

```
10.4 Graphics
10.4.1 My application complains about being unable to change the resolution or color depth
You generally need to edit the Screen section of your /etc/X11/xorg.conf to support additional color depths and resolutions. There may also be a problem with Xrandr.
```

Another method is to use Xephyr. See https://wiki.winehq.org/256_Color_Mode
So far adding this line to Launch Options works for me:

```
Xephyr :2 -ac -resizeable -screen 1280x720x16 & env DISPLAY=:2 %command%
```
Additionally need to add these registries to `steamapps/compatdata/1659050/pfx/user.reg` or use `protontricks`
to disable window decoration and window manager control
since there is no window manager running in Xephyr.
Running `mutter` or another WM in Xephyr seems to mess things up for me.
```
[Software\\Wine\\X11 Driver] 1639931810
#time=1d7f4f69fbce18e
"Decorated"="N"
"Managed"="N"
```
Going full screen and then back to windowed screen seems to break the game however. Audio still running though. `Alt+Enter` sometimes fixes the problem.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Touhou Fuujinroku ~ Mountain of Faith.
  - Japanese: 東方風神録　～ Mountain of Faith.
- Steam AppID of the game: 1100140

## System Information
- GPU: RX 560
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.0.3
- Kernel version: 5.11.0-37-generic
- Link to full system information (without personal data) report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/simon-i1-h/ba5bfd545c9d40befd11f7d3fbb1c197
- Proton version: 6.3-7

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1100140.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7285831/steam-1100140.log) (without personal data)

## Symptoms

When I played Touhou Fuujinroku using Steam Play, I found a problem that characters were misaligned such as dialog text because they were used the wrong font.

The Cause of the problem may be that Touhou series assumes that the system font is MS fonts (such as MS Gothic and MS Mincho).

The best solution is to bundle MS fonts to Steam Play, but you can also use another suitable font, such as the Ume font, which is the same size as MS fonts. However, other fonts may lack some glyphs that MS fonts have.

Ume font: https://osdn.net/projects/ume-font/

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Defiance
- Steam AppID of the game: N/A

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6900 XT (radeonsi, navi21, LLVM 19.1.7, DRM 3.61, 6.14.3-arch1-1) (0x73af)
- Video driver version: Arch Linux mesa 1:25.0.4-1
- Kernel version: 6.14.3-arch1-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/arazilsongweaver/11fbb1fcc69e85574b29e51136e25ee0
- Proton version: 6.3 (last known working version), Experimental (broken)

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Log Files

[protonexp-fail.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19880498/protonexp-fail.log)
[proton63-success.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19880499/proton63-success.log)

## Symptoms

Defiance does not connect to servers under the latest Proton Experimental but connects ok under Proton 6.3.

## Reproduction

[The following instructions use the latest stable build of Lutris and that Proton 6.3 is installed via Steam.]
1. Set the Lutris WINE version to "Proton 6.3"
1. Install Fawkes Launcher under Proton 6.3.
2. Use Fawkes Launcher to install Defiance under Proton 6.3.
4. Launch the Defiance launcher under Proton 6.3.
5. Verify operation. The game should connect and allow you to create a character after playing the introduction cutscene.
6. Close out of everything.
7. Configure Lutris to launch Defiance using Proton Experimental instead of Proton 6.3.
8. Launch the Defiance launcher under Proton Experimental.
9. Verify operation. The game will attempt to connect and then instantly return a message stating that the game is currently unavailable. There is no getting past this connection error message.

## Related Bugs
https://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=37813
